strengthens or weakens out of past experience and gives the
background, the second house expands or spreads forth the
personality laying out all the resources. Thus successful per
sons always have strong succeedent houses (2, 5, 8 and 11), as
the resourceful lady and Martin Luther have a strong second.
That is when the second house gets an emphasis from the
presence of a planet therein, it implies the real problem of
personal means.
Coming to the western hemisphere, we have to take the
seventh in connection with the sixth and the eighth. The
seventh focusses the relationships in which it is essential to
defer to others or work in co-operation with others. In other
words it involves a partnership or a co-responsible work. The
absence of a planet in the seventh implies no such obligations;
and Woodrow Wilson is a good example, for he was swept
along by destiny himself being never sensitive to anything of
that kind. The sixth house, therefore, on the analogy of the
